questions.text: Which tissue conducts water in flowering plants?
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water and dissolved minerals from root to shoot.

questions.text: Which tissue protects the plant and helps reduce water loss?
questions.explanation: The epidermis forms the outer protective layer of the plant body.

questions.text: Which meristem is responsible for secondary growth in dicot stems?
questions.explanation: Lateral meristem such as cambium adds girth to stems and roots.

questions.text: The vascular bundles in a dicot stem are arranged:
questions.explanation: Dicot stems have vascular bundles arranged in a ring around the pith.

questions.text: Which layer of the root is responsible for lateral root formation?
questions.explanation: The pericycle gives rise to lateral roots in dicot plants.

questions.text: Which tissue helps in support and is alive at maturity?
questions.explanation: Collenchyma provides flexible support and is living at maturity.

questions.text: The study of internal structure of plants is called:
questions.explanation: Anatomy deals with the organization and structure of tissues and organs.

questions.text: A group of cells having a common origin and usually performing a common function is called a:
questions.explanation: Tissues are classified into meristematic and permanent tissues.

questions.text: Meristems which appear early in life of a plant and contribute to the formation of the primary plant body are called:
questions.explanation: They include apical meristems and intercalary meristems.

questions.text: The meristem which occurs between mature tissues is known as:
questions.explanation: They occur in grasses and regenerate parts removed by the grazing herbivores.

questions.text: Which tissue is composed of cells that are generally isodiametric and perform functions like photosynthesis, storage and secretion?
questions.explanation: Parenchyma forms the major component within organs.

questions.text: Sclerenchyma consists of long, narrow cells with thick and lignified cell walls having a few or numerous pits. They are usually:
questions.explanation: They provide mechanical support to organs.

questions.text: Xylem is composed of four different kinds of elements, namely:
options: Sieve tubes, companion cells, phloem parenchyma, phloem fibres
Tracheids, vessels, xylem fibres, xylem parenchyma
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms lack vessels in their xylem.

questions.text: In primary xylem, the first formed xylem elements are called ________ and the later formed are called ________.
questions.explanation: In stems, protoxylem lies towards the centre (pith) and metaxylem lies towards the periphery (endarch condition).

questions.text: The cells of Phloem which are involved in transport of food and are closely associated with companion cells are:
questions.explanation: A mature sieve element possesses a peripheral cytoplasm and a large vacuole but lacks a nucleus.

questions.text: The epidermal hairs on the stem are called:
questions.explanation: Trichomes are usually multicellular and help in preventing water loss due to transpiration.

questions.text: In a dicot leaf, the tissue between the upper and the lower epidermis is called the:
questions.explanation: Mesophyll contains chloroplasts and carries out photosynthesis.

questions.text: In dicot stems, the cells between xylem and phloem which become meristematic are called:
questions.explanation: Intrafascicular cambium is a primary meristem present between primary xylem and primary phloem.

questions.text: The activity of cambium is under the control of many physiological and environmental factors. In temperate regions, the cambium is more active in spring, producing:
questions.explanation: Spring wood or early wood has xylem elements with wider vessels.

questions.text: The dark brown, hard and durable central region of secondary xylem that is resistant to microorganisms is:
questions.explanation: Heartwood (duramen) does not conduct water but gives mechanical support.

questions.text: The functional, peripheral region of secondary xylem which is lighter in colour and involved in water conduction is:
questions.explanation: Sapwood is also known as alburnum.

questions.text: The phellogen, phellem and phelloderm are collectively known as:
questions.explanation: Phellogen is cork cambium; phellem is cork; phelloderm is secondary cortex.

questions.text: Lenticels are lens-shaped openings in the bark that permit:
questions.explanation: They occur in most woody trees.

questions.text: Secondary growth occurs in:
questions.explanation: Most monocotyledons do not exhibit secondary growth.

questions.text: The ground tissue system consists of all tissues except:
questions.explanation: Ground tissue constitutes the bulk of the plant body.

questions.text: Vascular bundles in which cambium is present between phloem and xylem are called:
questions.explanation: Open bundles possess the ability to form secondary xylem and phloem tissues.

questions.text: In roots, xylem and phloem in a vascular bundle are arranged in an alternate manner along different radii, such an arrangement is called:
questions.explanation: Radial arrangement is characteristic of roots.

questions.text: The innermost layer of the cortex in dicot roots is called:
questions.explanation: It contains Casparian strips which are water-impermeable waxy material suberin.

questions.text: Stomata are regulated by two bean-shaped cells known as:
questions.explanation: In grasses, the guard cells are dumb-bell shaped.

questions.text: The bulliform cells in grasses help in:
questions.explanation: When they are flaccid due to water stress, they make the leaves curl inwards.

questions.text: In a dicot stem, the layer of cells just below the epidermis which provides mechanical strength to the young stem is:
questions.explanation: In dicots, hypodermis is collenchymatous.

questions.text: Monocot stems are characterized by the presence of:
questions.explanation: Each vascular bundle is surrounded by a sclerenchymatous bundle sheath.

questions.text: In a dorsiventral (dicot) leaf, the palisade parenchyma is located towards the:
questions.explanation: Adaxial is upper surface; abaxial is lower surface.

questions.text: Isobilateral leaves are characteristic of:
questions.explanation: In an isobilateral leaf, the stomata are present on both the surfaces of the epidermis.

questions.text: Primary xylem and primary phloem are formed by:
questions.explanation: They are part of the primary growth of the plant.

questions.text: Wood is actually:
questions.explanation: Continuous activity of the vascular cambium adds layers of secondary xylem.

questions.text: Annual rings are formed by the combination of:
questions.explanation: One annual ring represents the growth of one year.

questions.text: In dicot roots, the vascular cambium is entirely ________ in origin.
questions.explanation: It originates from the tissue located just below the phloem bundles and a portion of pericycle tissue.

questions.text: The conjunctive tissue is found in:
questions.explanation: The parenchymatous cells which lie between the xylem and the phloem in roots are called conjunctive tissue.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT a lateral meristem?
questions.explanation: Intercalary meristem is a primary meristem.
